A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.

2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:17:59.120820292Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.

- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:184:{"acceptance":"1. TestExecuteBeadRejectsTopLevelSampleChildBeads simulates an execute-bead Step 0 agent running `ddx bead create "Sample: lint cleanup"` without parent metadata and proves the real tracker is not polluted with an open top-level bead.
2. TestExecuteBeadDecompositionRequiresCurrentParent creates a legitimate child through the execute-bead decomposition path and proves the child parent equals the current bead ID, labels/spec-id are inherited, and invalid parentless children are rejected or quarantined.
3. TestWorkStatusDropsStaleRunStateWhenWorkerPidGone creates a run-state record for a dead worker pid and proves `ddx work status --json` does not report it as active work after cleanup.
4. Invalid execute-bead child creation emits durable operator_attention evidence with child IDs, attempted title, parent value, and rejection reason.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadRejectsTopLevelSampleChildBeads|TestExecuteBeadDecompositionRequiresCurrentParent|TestWorkStatusDropsStaleRunStateWhenWorkerPidGone' passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"1b41b22bba8872069672d35d4ebffeb6ef795573","created_at":"2026-06-13T16:16:29.347711662Z","description":"PROBLEM
During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-aef128f7, an interrupted `ddx work` execution polluted the real DDx tracker with 20 top-level open beads titled `Sample: ...` (`ddx-5d26dc27`, `ddx-37dd1bef`, `ddx-e7f8ad72`, `ddx-2a77ac3f`, `ddx-d369d255`, `ddx-9646569a`, `ddx-3cf17faf`, `ddx-541bf722`, `ddx-e9384053`, `ddx-2a5286ae`, `ddx-fb8f9a90`, `ddx-552ec9c5`, `ddx-86e2a4ce`, `ddx-0b369a91`, `ddx-2eed7ae3`, `ddx-8c6abcc2`, `ddx-e64dea54`, `ddx-6479485f`, `ddx-8fbd3aba`, `ddx-10c17c2d`). I cancelled those bogus beads through `ddx bead update --status cancelled`, but the worker should never have been able to create them in the landing tracker.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1885-1903 tells execute-bead agents that Step 0 may run `ddx bead create` for decomposition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1981-1984 reiterates that Step 0 allows bead creation while current-bead lifecycle stays orchestrator-owned. The prompt requires `parent=<parent-id>`, but DDx does not enforce that an execute-bead-created child has the current bead as parent, inherits required labels/spec-id, or writes a valid `no_changes_rationale.txt` before mutating the real landing tracker. The dogfood attempt created top-level sample fixtures instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guarded decomposition command/path for execute-bead Step 0, or validate all bead-create mutations made from an execution worktree before they reach the landing tracker.
- Reject or quarantine execute-bead-created beads whose title/description/AC look like sample fixtures, whose parent is empty or not the current bead, or whose required inherited labels/spec-id are absent.
- Add cleanup/recovery that parks or cancels invalid child beads automatically and records an operator_attention event naming the invalid IDs.
- Ensure stale run-state from a killed worker does not keep `ddx work status` reporting active work after the owning worker process is gone.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove legitimate Step 0 decomposition. Do not rewrite or delete tracker history manually. Do not change normal operator-invoked `ddx bead create` behavior outside execute-bead/work execution contexts.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: the same aborted run also proved process-tree cleanup failures.
- ddx-6d9a8bdb: the same run also proved pinned-provider escape.","id":"ddx-42124c23","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:beads","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-90","schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"work: prevent execute-bead from polluting queue with sample child beads","triage.estimated_difficulty":"hard","updated_at":"2026-06-13T17:11:12.293723582Z"}

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2848` implements `Store.DepAdd` with existence, self-edge, and dependency-cycle checks only; it never walks `Bead.Parent` ancestry before appending the dependency at `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2888`. `cli/cmd/bead.go:328` wires `ddx bead create` and accepts `--parent` at `cli/cmd/bead.go:355`, but creation-time dependency handling does not share a parent-ancestor invariant with `DepAdd`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a central tracker validation helper that builds the parent chain for the dependent bead or pending create request and hard-rejects any dependency whose target is the bead's parent or any ancestor. Use the helper from `Store.DepAdd` and from `ddx bead create --depends-on ... --parent ...` handling. Error text must name the offending ancestor and include the parent chain in stable order. Preserve normal dependency cycle detection for non-parent graph cycles.

NON-SCOPE: Do not change decomposition semantics for valid `parent -> child` edges. Do not auto-repair existing tracker rows in this bead; repair belongs to the doctor bead. Do not change work-loop retry policy or root-mutation logging. Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:63` defines decomposed parent state; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:182` defines the decomposition-first transition sequence.

Operators need an explicit startup signal so stale TLS or tsnet state does not keep confusing client/server trust debugging.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` previously allowed `~/.ddx/` to act as a project state root. High-risk server consumers include TLS cert/key state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:326` and tsnet state at `cli/internal/server/server.go:503`. Once the resolver falls through to XDG, those old home directories are no longer read by the server path helpers.

PROPOSED FIX: Add startup detection in the server path that checks for legacy `JoinHome("server")` and `JoinHome("tsnet")` directories when the active project state root resolves elsewhere. Emit a clear warning naming the orphan source and the active XDG/project state destination. Keep the behavior warning-only unless the implementation already has an established safe migration helper.

NON-SCOPE: Do not rename `~/.ddx/` to `~/.config/ddx/`. Do not address multi-project federation collisions. Do not change TLS-renew scheduling or rotation logic. Do not silently delete legacy home-state directories. .ddx/beads.jsonl:232:{"acceptance":"1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with exported Path(ctx, projectRoot) function.
2. TestDDxRoot_DefaultsToProjectRootDotDDx asserts Path returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx") in the default (no XDG fallback yet) case.
3. Static check: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. This is the regression guard for the audit. (The check is wired into lefthook as a pre-commit rule.)
4. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. All existing tests pass without modification.
5. The audit covers at minimum the 12 path-writers enumerated in the description. The bead's no_changes_rationale.txt (if used) MUST list every file modified by the audit, grouped by package.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes, including the new "no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths" rule.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:08:53.863138043Z","description":"PROBLEM
Every place in the codebase that writes to .ddx/<something> today hardcodes filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...). This makes it impossible to relocate the DDx state to an XDG location for convention-mode projects without auditing and rewriting every call site. A leaked path means the convention-mode invariant ("no files written to project tree without a .ddx/ folder") breaks the moment that code path runs.

The full enumeration of touched paths is at minimum:
- .ddx/config.yaml
- .ddx/beads.jsonl
- .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
- .ddx/attachments/<id>/
- .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
- .ddx/run-state/<id>.json
- .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
- .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
- .ddx/plugins/<name>/
- .ddx/workers/<id>/
- .ddx/agent-logs/
- .ddx/scratch/
- and likely 10+ more across cli/internal/server/, cli/cmd/, library/checks/, scripts/

ROOT CAUSE
There is no single accessor that returns "the DDx root for this project". cli/internal/config/ has GetMetaPrompt() and similar config getters, but no DDxRoot(ctx) function. Every path-writer reaches into the file system directly.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a new package cli/internal/ddxroot with a single public function: `Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string`. Today returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"); a follow-up bead (E2) extends it to fall back to XDG when projectRoot/.ddx does not exist.
2. Audit every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment. Replace `filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...)` with `filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), ...)`. The audit must cover cli/cmd/, cli/internal/, library/checks/ (where applicable to ddx-side paths).
3. Add a build-time linter check (or a pre-commit hook lint rule) that fails the build if a new `filepath.Join.*"\\.ddx"` literal appears outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Use go vet via a custom analyzer OR a simple ripgrep-based pre-commit hook (lefthook).
4. The resolver MUST NOT change behaviour in this bead: for any projectRoot today, ddxroot.Path returns exactly filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"). This bead is a pure refactor — no functional change.
5. Existing tests must continue to pass without modification. If any test fails, it has hardcoded .ddx in a way that bypassed the resolver — fix that test.

NON-SCOPE
- XDG fallback path (E2 bead).
- Convention mode bootstrap (E2 bead).
- `ddx init --global` (E3 bead).
- Manifest overlay (E5 bead).
- Migration of existing data (none required; behaviour is identical).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor that the rest of the epic builds on.","id":"ddx-564ea1cf","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:refactor","kind:refactor","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint"],"notes":"Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. No performance baseline exists for the documents/artifacts UI at realistic corpus size (2000 artifacts). Without a baseline, the caching work (ddx-4a7eed8c) cannot demonstrate improvement, and CI has no reference point for regression detection.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/ does not contain a scale fixture for 2000 artifacts.
- No measurement of first-paint cold/warm latency, scroll smoothness, or search latency at this scale has been recorded.
- ddx-b9993722 (FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract, a dep) established the measurement methodology; this bead applies it to the 2k scale.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).
- Record baseline measurements in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md.
- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T04:42:58.012634526Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T043749-7c9fea7a","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":5277637,"output_tokens":29436,"total_tokens":5307073,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":302515,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T04:42:58.277858239Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=5307073 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-06T04:43:18.234874743Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|PreClaim|RetryEscalation|PreClaimDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. Operator decision: load Inter (do NOT introduce a new display font — that would be a spec change).

Add Inter via `@fontsource-variable/inter` (`bun add`), import in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/app.css`, so all non-mono roles render Inter instead of system fallback.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:360:{"acceptance":"1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":600,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.795710244Z"}

The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260506T120844-d2a3f066","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":1670655,"output_tokens":10807,"total_tokens":1681462,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":168678,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1681462 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"failure_class=review-error: transport

rationale: status: proposed
reason: Test infrastructure dependencies prevent completing deletions without extensive test rewrites

## Analysis

The 19 unreachable symbols fall into three categories:

1. **DUPLICATE FUNCTIONS (11)** - Superseded by attempt.go implementations:
   - ratelimit_retry.go: EvaluateRateLimitWait, IsRateLimitResult, hasIsolated429, ParseRetryAfter, ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr, RateLimitRetryConfig.resolved, ctxSleep, RunWithRateLimitRetry, BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt

2. **ORPHANED FUNCTIONS (2)** - Never called from production or tests:
   - service_run.go: TestProviderConnectivityViaService, ValidateEffortForRunViaService
   - profile_select.go: WarmProfileSnapshotForProject

3. **PRODUCTION INFRASTRUCTURE (6)** - Used in production paths but test-utilities:
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: NewPreClaimIntakeHook (used from workers.go)
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult (test wrapper)
   - profile_select.go: SelectCheapestProfile (test utility)
   - recovery_decompose.go: NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - recovery_reframe.go: NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - triage_dispatch.go: IsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure (test utility)

## Blocker

Deleting categories #2 and #3 breaks 15+ test functions that specifically test these deleted functions. The tests cannot be easily updated because:
- Many tests directly call these functions (not through mocks)
- Some test utilities (like decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult) are called from 6+ test functions
- Removing test utilities would eliminate test coverage of edge cases

## Recommendation

This work should be split into:
1. **Phase A (Immediate)**: Delete only the 11 duplicate ratelimit_retry functions (with corresponding test file deletion)
2. **Phase B (Follow-up bead)**: Decide on test utility functions (keep as test-only or delete+refactor all tests)
3. **Phase C (Follow-up bead)**: Wire production infrastructure functions (NewPreClaimIntakeHook, hooks) into CLI reachability graph

AC #2 requirement (no deadcode hits for these files) cannot be met without deleting test utilities, which breaks AC #5 (tests must pass).

## Summary

Operator review needed:
- Accept PENDING status for test utilities and production infrastructure
- Create Phase B bead for test utility disposition
- Create Phase C bead for hooking infrastructure into production entry points

The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"codex","resolved_model":"gpt-5.4-mini","fallback_chain":[],"actual_power":8}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260505T164659-abc25e07","harness":"codex","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","input_tokens":285426,"output_tokens":2755,"total_tokens":288181,"cost_usd":0,"duration_ms":34343,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=288181 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"The bead is too large for this execution depth and cannot be cleanly split further here. .ddx/beads.jsonl:422:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` adds self-contained Niflheim evidence cases for both observed details: `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`; it asserts the selected action is reconcile, retry-land, preserve-evidence, or operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run.
2. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` proves land/ref/index/evidence-staging outcomes do not raise requested min power/profile and do not request stronger model escalation.
3. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, land/reconcile status, and any review status available for the land coordination scenarios.
4. Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.

Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`.   cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1
However the required broad command failed reproducibly in unrelated cmd package tests:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1
and staged pre-commit failed in go-test with the same unrelated cmd failures plus an unrelated internal/agent short-race failure:
  lefthook run pre-commit
Representative failures include TestBeadDoctorDetectsParentAncestorBackEdge, TestDoctor_ReportsAndAppliesHousekeepingCounts, TestDoctor_ReportsBothInstallLayers, TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence*, TestRunPersonaInjectsBodyIntoPrompt, TestSkillsCheckDefaultPathsIncludesShippedPluginSkills, TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence, and TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt. The in-scope land coordination acceptance tests are green, but the bead contract forbids committing while required gates are red.

A Niflheim decomposed parent bead kept consuming attempts and escalated to stronger routes despite evidence that the bead was already split into executable children. Stronger models cannot implement a parent bead whose correct action is decompose/execution-ineligible/operator work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing or empty decomposition paths can warn and continue.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` recognizes execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says only genuine failure should escalate, but decomposed-parent/no-changes outcomes can still reach implementation retry and min-power escalation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style decomposed-parent fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`, `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`. Classify the parent as execution-ineligible, proposed, dependency-waiting, or operator-attention according to policy before another implementation attempt is scheduled. Preserve child bead evidence in the event/result audit.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related precedent: `ddx-e1a576a7` for execution-ineligible decomposed parent behavior. Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.","events_attachment":"ddx-d2725150/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-d2725150","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-334c0958","priority":0,"queue-rank":2100,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e413b018","status":"closed","title":"Mark decomposed parent beads execution-ineligible before retry","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:36.039852043Z"}

A parent bead that has already been decomposed can still re-enter the worker as if it were an executable task. That keeps spending attempts on a structurally ineligible parent instead of moving on to its children.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-210` already marks decomposed children with `Parent=beadID` and sets the parent execution-eligible flag to false, but the main worker loop still has paths that continue past decomposition-like intake and retry handling. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles some intake decomposition cases, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3818-3840` continues processing other outcome branches without consistently parking a parent whose own `no_changes` rationale says to decompose.

PROPOSED FIX
- When the worker sees `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`, mark the parent execution-ineligible or proposed before another implementation attempt.
- Do not schedule another implementation run or min-power escalation for that parent.
- Keep the parent visible as a decomposition boundary, not as a retriable implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change child-bead content, land handling, or review gating in this child.
- Do not alter the decomposer output format beyond what is needed to detect the decomposed-parent state.","events_attachment":"ddx-d4817c9b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-d4817c9b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-334c0958","priority":0,"queue-rank":2050,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5e04821f","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: park decomposed parents before retrying implementation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:35.835830846Z"}

3. `applyPreClaimDecomposition` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, `runDecomposer` in `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go`, and `AutoDecomposeEpic` in `cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go` close the decomposed parent through the lifecycle API after children are created, preserve child IDs in durable event/extra evidence, and clear claim metadata/heartbeat from the parent.
4. `TestIntake_TooLargeDecomposed_CreatesChildrenAndClosesParent` proves pre-claim decomposition creates children, closes the parent, records `triage-decomposed`, and leaves the children in `ReadyExecution` when they have no explicit deps.
5. `TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_ClosesParentAndLeavesChildrenReady` proves post-attempt orchestrator decomposition closes the parent and does not block generated children through the `Parent` field.
6. `TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge_ClosesParent` proves the post-ladder decomposer path closes the parent and records `decompose-applied` with child IDs.
7. `TestAutoDecomposeEpic_HappyPathClosesParent` or equivalent proves the auto-epic-decompose path closes the parent and still emits `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence.
8. Existing back-edge coverage remains or is extended: `TestDecomposeDoesNotSelfDep` (or equivalent) asserts every generated child excludes the parent ID from `DepIDs()`, and `ddx bead doctor` parent/ancestor dep tests still pass if present.

11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-06-08T04:01:54.619661516Z","description":"PROBLEM
DDx decomposition guidance and implementation currently frame decomposed parents as open, execution-ineligible containers. That leaves stale open parent beads in the queue and makes the safe operational answer unclear when an agent splits a bead: the correct answer is that a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition and should be closed. The downstream invariant is that children must still be work-ready: `parent` is hierarchy metadata, not a dependency gate, and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children, and docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 forbid `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the current bead and only allow a parent-note update during Step 0 decomposition, so agent-facing instructions do not say to mark a decomposed bead completed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2734-2743 logs successful pre-claim decomposition then releases the parent while explicitly commenting that it stays open until children close. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5248-5275 implements `applyPreClaimDecomposition` by setting `execution-eligible=false` instead of closing the parent.
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-133 and 207-210, plus cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go:182-204, use the same park-open pattern.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go:73-77 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go:20-22 assert the old open/ineligible behavior, so tests lock in the ambiguous framing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the decomposition lifecycle contract in docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and FEAT-010 where applicable: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers may remain as legacy/backfill cases, but they are not the normative path for new decomposition.
- Update .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go, and prompt snapshots so Step 0 says: agents may create child/sibling/replacement beads, wire only child-to-child or sibling/replacement deps, must not add the parent as a child dependency, and must close the decomposed parent once the split is lossless and durable.
- Update `applyPreClaimDecomposition`, `runDecomposer`, and `AutoDecomposeEpic` to close the parent with a lifecycle transition after child creation. Preserve traceability via `triage-decomposed` / `decompose-applied` events and child IDs in Extra or event body. Clear claim metadata/heartbeat so a closed parent cannot remain half-owned.
- Preserve downstream readiness semantics: children are created with `Parent=<decomposed-id>` but no dependency on the parent. Existing child-to-child dependency ordering remains allowed. Closing the parent must not be used as a substitute for adding or removing legitimate child-to-child deps.
- Update and add tests around the changed lifecycle behavior and the child readiness invariant.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b (ddx work idle-path diagnose and auto-remediate). This bead clarifies one remediation path so the queue advances instead of preserving dead intermediates.
- Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab handles parent/ancestor dependency back-edge prevention and repair. This bead handles the higher-level lifecycle contract: close the decomposed parent and prove `Parent` metadata does not block children.

PARENT AND DEPS

The Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 Playwright test is already implemented at
cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1164-1429 and satisfies every AC
in this bead. No source change is required:

AC 1 — fixture shape:
  Lines 1165-1183 build TOOL_COUNT=200 tool calls and EVIDENCE_COUNT=30
  evidence files for the mocked GraphQL responses.

AC 2 — tab render + one-rAF interactivity:
  - prompt tab: line 1382 click, line 1383-1385 content assertion,
    line 1386 `expect(await nextFrameOk()).toBe(true)`.
  - response tab: line 1390 click, line 1391-1393 content assertion,
    line 1394 nextFrameOk guard.
  - tools tab: line 1399 click, line 1400-1411 paginates through all
    200 entries with a nextFrameOk guard after each page (lines 1402,
    1406).
  - evidence tab: line 1415 click, line 1417 asserts all 30 paths,
    line 1418 asserts the last path, line 1419 nextFrameOk; lines
    1420-1427 inline-view round-trip with a final nextFrameOk.
  - `nextFrameOk` (lines 1372-1379) wraps `requestAnimationFrame` and
    asserts the single-frame delta < 100ms, satisfying the "interactive
    within one requestAnimationFrame" contract.

AC 3 — `bun run test:e2e` passes:
  Verified locally in this worktree by:
    cd cli/internal/server/frontend && \
      XDG_DATA_HOME=$(mktemp -d -t ddx-e2e-xdg-XXXX) \
      bun run test:e2e -- --grep "Story16RunDetailFullStackP95" --reporter=line
  Result: "1 passed (14.1s)" for the Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 test.
  (XDG_DATA_HOME override is required only on hosts where a long-lived
  ddx server already holds the per-machine singleton lock; CI does not
  need it.)

AC 4 — `cd cli && go test ./...`:

axon_schema::schema::{CollectionSchema, CollectionView};\n\nuse crate::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n/// PostgreSQL-backed storage adapter.\n///\n/// Uses the asynchronous `tokio-postgres` crate driven from a dedicated Tokio\n/// runtime. Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\n///\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\n/// exist.\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n    in_tx: bool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\n    ///\n    /// Example: `\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\"`\n    pub fn connect(params: &str) -> Result<Self, AxonError> {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n            .worker_threads(2)\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\n        let (client, connection) = handle\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\"connection failed: {e}\")))?;\n        handle.spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\n                tracing::error!(\"postgres connection error: {e}\");\n            }\n        });\n        let mut adapter = Self {\n            client,\n            handle,\n            _rt: rt,\n            in_tx: false,\n        };\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\n        Ok(adapter)\n    }\n\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\n    ///\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime.  This\n    /// prevents the \"cannot call `block_on` from within an async context\"\n    /// panic that would otherwise occur when gateway route handlers drive\n    /// storage calls.\n    ///\n    /// Requires the multi-thread Tokio scheduler (used by the production\n    /// server).  Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\n    fn block<T>(\n        &self,\n        fut: impl Future<Output = Result<T, tokio_postgres::Error>>,\n    ) -> Result<T, AxonError> {\n        match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n            Ok(_) => tokio::task::block_in_place(|| self.handle.block_on(fut)),\n            Err(_) => self.handle.block_on(fut),\n        }\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))\n    }\n\n    fn init_schema(&mut self) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        self.block(self.client.batch_execute(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS databases (\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS namespaces (\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES databases(name) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, name)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entities (\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\n                    id            TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    version       BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    data          JSONB NOT NULL,\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, id)\n                );\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schemas (\n id             BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n                    timestamp_ns   BIGINT NOT NULL,\n                    collection     TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    entity_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n
